name: Bárcena Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Bárcena – circular completa desde San Miguel de las Dueñas
description: This difficult 25.0-mile (40.2 km) touring bicycle route around Embalse de Bárcena offers scenic views and 2250 feet (686 metres) of elevati
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Congosto traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by river valleys, significant reservoirs, and rolling terrain. The region encompasses areas with varied natural features, including the large Bárcena reservoir in León and the Henares and Bornova river landscapes in Guadalajara. These areas offer a mix of open roads and paths alongside water bodies, providing a range of cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Congosto The most popular touring cycling route is Bárcena Reservoir Loop, a 25.0 miles (40.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route circles the expansive Bárcena reservoir, offering views of the water and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Boeza Bridge – Ponferrada Castle loop from Ponferrada, a difficult 16.0 miles (25.7 km) path. This route includes sections along the Boeza River and passes by historical landmarks such as Ponferrada Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Compostilla – ️ Parish of Compostilla loop from aparcamiento, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Congosto is defined by river valleys, large reservoirs, and historical sites, with routes available for various ability levels. The routes in Congosto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Congosto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Congosto?
A: Congosto offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 160 different tours available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Congosto?
A: The touring cycling routes in Congosto are predominantly challenging, with 97 routes rated as difficult and 56 as moderate. For those seeking a gentler ride, there are 8 easy routes available.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Congosto?
A: While many routes in Congosto are more challenging, there are 8 easy touring cycling routes available. These are ideal for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. One moderate option that might be suitable for those building experience is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31523364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Blas and San Roque – Compostilla loop from Cubillos del Sil&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 22 km.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Congosto?
A: The routes in Congosto offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. In the León province, you can cycle around the expansive Bárcena reservoir or explore areas near historical landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/317309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6190464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinaseca Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The Guadalajara region features river landscapes, particularly around the Henares and Bornova rivers, and remnants of its rich mining heritage.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Congosto?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Congosto are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish in the same location. Popular circular routes include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035185259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.2 km long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Compostilla – ⭐️ Parish of Compostilla loop from aparcamiento&lt;/a&gt;, covering 32.6 km.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Congosto?
A: The touring cycling routes in Congosto are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to large reservoirs, and the opportunity to encounter historical sites along the way.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the touring cycling routes in Congosto?
A: Parking availability varies by specific starting points. Many towns and villages in the Congosto region, especially those serving as popular access points for outdoor activities, typically offer public parking areas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking at the trailhead.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Congosto using public transport?
A: Access to touring cycling routes in Congosto via public transport can be limited, particularly for more remote trailheads. Larger towns like Ponferrada (near the León Congosto) may offer bus or train connections, but reaching specific starting points for cycling tours often requires personal vehicle transport. Always check local transport schedules and bike carriage policies in advance.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Congosto?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Congosto pass through or near villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and pubs. Especially around the Bárcena reservoir in León or along the Henares River in Guadalajara, you'll find opportunities to refuel and relax. For example, routes near Ponferrada or Molinaseca offer several options.

Q: What are some notable historical sites to explore while cycling in Congosto?
A: Congosto is rich in history. In the León area, you can visit the 10th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/317309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponferrada Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the Monastery of San Miguel de las Dueñas, or the Church of Almázcara with its Roman milestones. In Guadalajara, explore the 10th-century bridge over the Henares River or learn about the region's mining past at the 'El País de la Plata' Interpretation Center.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Congosto?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Congosto generally spans from spring to autumn. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, while autumn provides cooler weather and beautiful foliage. Summer can be warm, especially in the river valleys, but routes near reservoirs like Bárcena or Cañamares offer refreshing breaks. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that feature significant water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The Congosto region is known for its prominent water features. In León, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035185259" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bárcena Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, circling the large Bárcena reservoir. In Guadalajara, routes often follow the Henares River or pass by the Cañamares river reservoir dam and the Bornova River Reservoir, offering scenic views and potential resting spots.
tips: Nice asphalt track with an irregular surface but a wonderful perspective of the River Sil as it widens to fill the Barcena reservoir. It is cut off to car traffic due to landslides.

Cubillos is a town halfway between urban and rural, with extensive irrigated crops fed by the Bárcena reservoir, but with a marked presence of the Compostilla II Thermal Power Plant, in operation from 1972 to June 2020.

Road next to the reservoir that also borders the Compostilla II Thermal Power Plant. This plant was fueled by coal and was cooled by the water from the reservoir. It was closed and disconnected from the electricity grid in June 2020 due to its high polluting impact.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed as a complete circular loop starting and ending in San Miguel de las Dueñas. While a specific, formally designated trailhead isn't documented, you can typically find suitable parking within the village to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas or consult local maps upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling around Embalse de Bárcena?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Embalse de Bárcena and in the El Bierzo region is varied. For touring cycling, you can expect a mix of paved roads and potentially some well-maintained dirt or gravel tracks, depending on the exact path chosen. The roads directly bordering the reservoir are generally suitable for pleasant cycling. Be prepared for undulating terrain with gradual climbs and descents, as the area is surrounded by mountains.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of approximately 40 km and an elevation gain of around 685 meters, this route is rated as difficult. While the area offers routes for various fitness levels, this specific circular route from San Miguel de las Dueñas would require good fitness and some cycling experience due to its length and elevation changes. Beginners might find it challenging.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Embalse de Bárcena cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers continuous scenic views of the Embalse de Bárcena and the surrounding El Bierzo region. Key highlights include the Santa Marina del Sil Road and Views of the Sil River, the charming settlement of Cubillos de Sil, and a prominent View of the Bárcena Reservoir and the Compostilla II power plant. You'll also enjoy vistas of surrounding mountains and verdant valleys.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The El Bierzo region, where Embalse de Bárcena is located, is known for its natural beauty, which is best appreciated in good natural light. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for cycling. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder weather, though cycling is possible year-round depending on conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Bárcena circular cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows roads and potentially some tracks, the general area is welcoming to dogs. If you plan to bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved and accustomed to cycling alongside you or in a trailer. Always keep them on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas, and be mindful of local regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Embalse de Bárcena circular route from San Miguel de las Dueñas. The roads and paths are generally open for public access.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Olvidado (Cirujales-Cacabelos) and the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón. Smaller portions also overlap with the Senda de los Canteros, Camino Natural de la Ruta de la Conquista, and Senda de Bas, among others.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Bierzo region is well-equipped with services for visitors. San Miguel de las Dueñas, your starting point, and other towns like Cubillos de Sil along the route, offer various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. Ponferrada, a larger town nearby, also provides extensive services and is easily accessible.

faqs.question: What makes this route notable for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is notable for its tranquil waterside cycling along the Embalse de Bárcena, offering stunning backdrops of the El Bierzo landscape. It's nestled in a region rich with history and culture, including proximity to the Camino de Santiago. The area is considered bike-friendly, providing a peaceful environment and a variety of experiences, from paved roads to well-maintained gravel paths, allowing for an adjustable level of adventure.
